kaos_elements.name: Safety Goal
kaos_elements.description: Safety objective for ATM: Each transaction must be recorded, and the client must have the ability to review all transactions performed against a given account
kaos_elements.measurable_criteria: Quality-attribute specific requirement analysis

kaos_elements.name: Safety Requirement 1
kaos_elements.description: The system shall ensure safety (hazard prevention): Each transaction must be recorded, and the client must have the ability to review all transactions performed against a given account
kaos_elements.measurable_criteria: Clause-level quality requirement for hazard prevention

kaos_elements.name: Safety Requirement 2
kaos_elements.description: The system shall ensure safety (fault tolerance): Recorded transactions must include the date, time, transaction type, amount and account balance after the transaction
kaos_elements.measurable_criteria: Clause-level quality requirement for fault tolerance

kaos_elements.name: Safety Requirement 3
kaos_elements.description: The system shall ensure safety (risk mitigation): A bank client can have two types of accounts
kaos_elements.measurable_criteria: Clause-level quality requirement for risk mitigation

kaos_elements.name: Safety Requirement 4
kaos_elements.description: The system shall ensure safety (hazard prevention): A checking-account and a saving-account
kaos_elements.measurable_criteria: Clause-level quality requirement for hazard prevention

kaos_elements.name: Safety Requirement 5
kaos_elements.description: The system shall ensure safety (fault tolerance): For each checking account, one related saving-account can exists
kaos_elements.measurable_criteria: Clause-level quality requirement for fault tolerance

kaos_elements.name: Safety Requirement 6
kaos_elements.description: The system shall ensure safety (risk mitigation): The application must verify that a client can gain access to his or her account by identification via a personal identification number (PIN) code
kaos_elements.measurable_criteria: Clause-level quality requirement for risk mitigation

kaos_elements.name: Safety Requirement 7
kaos_elements.description: The system shall ensure safety (hazard prevention): Neither a checking-account nor a saving-account can have a negative balance
kaos_elements.measurable_criteria: Clause-level quality requirement for hazard prevention

steps.analysis_text: Round 1: SafetyAgent submits model for dialectic review by EfficiencyAgent.
steps.analysis: Round 1: SafetyAgent submits model for dialectic review by EfficiencyAgent.

kaos_elements.name: Efficiency Goal
kaos_elements.description: Efficiency objective for ATM: A bank client can have two types of accounts
kaos_elements.measurable_criteria: Quality-attribute specific requirement analysis

kaos_elements.name: Efficiency Requirement 1
kaos_elements.description: The system shall ensure efficiency (latency optimization): A bank client can have two types of accounts
kaos_elements.measurable_criteria: Clause-level quality requirement for latency optimization

kaos_elements.name: Efficiency Requirement 2
kaos_elements.description: The system shall ensure efficiency (throughput stability): A checking-account and a saving-account
kaos_elements.measurable_criteria: Clause-level quality requirement for throughput stability

kaos_elements.name: Efficiency Requirement 3
kaos_elements.description: The system shall ensure efficiency (resource utilization): For each checking account, one related saving-account can exists
kaos_elements.measurable_criteria: Clause-level quality requirement for resource utilization

kaos_elements.name: Efficiency Requirement 4
kaos_elements.description: The system shall ensure efficiency (latency optimization): The application must verify that a client can gain access to his or her account by identification via a personal identification number (PIN) code
kaos_elements.measurable_criteria: Clause-level quality requirement for latency optimization

kaos_elements.name: Efficiency Requirement 5
kaos_elements.description: The system shall ensure efficiency (throughput stability): Neither a checking-account nor a saving-account can have a negative balance
kaos_elements.measurable_criteria: Clause-level quality requirement for throughput stability

kaos_elements.name: Efficiency Requirement 6
kaos_elements.description: The system shall ensure efficiency (resource utilization): The application should automatically withdraw funds from a related saving-account if the requested withdrawal amount on the checking-account is more than its current balance
kaos_elements.measurable_criteria: Clause-level quality requirement for resource utilization

kaos_elements.name: Sustainability Requirement 1
kaos_elements.description: The system shall ensure sustainability (energy footprint reduction): For each checking account, one related saving-account can exists

kaos_elements.name: Sustainability Requirement 2
kaos_elements.description: The system shall ensure sustainability (resource lifecycle control): The application must verify that a client can gain access to his or her account by identification via a personal identification number (PIN) code

kaos_elements.name: Trustworthiness Goal
kaos_elements.description: Trustworthiness objective for ATM: Neither a checking-account nor a saving-account can have a negative balance
kaos_elements.measurable_criteria: Quality-attribute specific requirement analysis

kaos_elements.name: Trustworthiness Requirement 1
kaos_elements.description: The system shall ensure trustworthiness (security assurance): Neither a checking-account nor a saving-account can have a negative balance
kaos_elements.measurable_criteria: Clause-level quality requirement for security assurance

kaos_elements.name: Trustworthiness Requirement 2
kaos_elements.description: The system shall ensure trustworthiness (auditability): The application should automatically withdraw funds from a related saving-account if the requested withdrawal amount on the checking-account is more than its current balance
kaos_elements.measurable_criteria: Clause-level quality requirement for auditability

kaos_elements.name: Trustworthiness Requirement 3
kaos_elements.description: The system shall ensure trustworthiness (integrity guarantees): If the saving-account balance is insufficient to cover the requested withdrawal amount, the application should inform the user and terminate the transaction
kaos_elements.measurable_criteria: Clause-level quality requirement for integrity guarantees

kaos_elements.name: Trustworthiness Requirement 4
kaos_elements.description: The system shall ensure trustworthiness (security assurance): The bank client must be able to deposit an amount to and withdraw an amount from his or her accounts using the bank application
kaos_elements.measurable_criteria: Clause-level quality requirement for security assurance

kaos_elements.name: Trustworthiness Requirement 5
kaos_elements.description: The system shall ensure trustworthiness (auditability): Each transaction must be recorded, and the client must have the ability to review all transactions performed against a given account
kaos_elements.measurable_criteria: Clause-level quality requirement for auditability

steps.analysis_text: The requirements for trustworthiness and responsibility in the bank application are well-defined, covering key aspects such as security, auditability, integrity, regulatory accountability, stakeholder transparency, and ethical compliance. However, there are overlaps between focus and reviewer elements, particularly regarding transaction recording and account management.
steps.analysis: The requirements for trustworthiness and responsibility in the bank application are well-defined, covering key aspects such as security, auditability, integrity, regulatory accountability, stakeholder transparency, and ethical compliance. However, there are overlaps between focus and reviewer elements, particularly regarding transaction recording and account management.
steps.feedback: Consider consolidating overlapping requirements to streamline the negotiation process. For instance, both trustworthiness and responsibility elements address transaction recording; a unified requirement could improve clarity.

steps.analysis_text: The negotiation between TrustworthinessAgent and ResponsibilityAgent revolves around the essential functions of the bank application concerning client transactions. The focus is on ensuring that all transactions are secure, auditable, and transparent while adhering to ethical and regulatory standards. Both agents have overlapping requirements that need to be addressed to ensure a cohesive understanding of the system's functionality.
steps.analysis: The negotiation between TrustworthinessAgent and ResponsibilityAgent revolves around the essential functions of the bank application concerning client transactions. The focus is on ensuring that all transactions are secure, auditable, and transparent while adhering to ethical and regulatory standards. Both agents have overlapping requirements that need to be addressed to ensure a cohesive understanding of the system's functionality.
steps.feedback: It is crucial to align the focus elements with the reviewer elements to ensure that trustworthiness and responsibility are maintained throughout the transaction process. Clear definitions and guidelines must be established for the handling of insufficient funds, recording of transactions, and account management.

steps.analysis_text: The focus elements outline the responsibilities of the banking application, ensuring that clients can manage their accounts effectively while adhering to ethical and regulatory standards. The reviewer elements emphasize safety measures to protect client transactions and account integrity. Both sets of elements are aligned in their goals, focusing on accountability and well-documented transactions.
steps.analysis: The focus elements outline the responsibilities of the banking application, ensuring that clients can manage their accounts effectively while adhering to ethical and regulatory standards. The reviewer elements emphasize safety measures to protect client transactions and account integrity. Both sets of elements are aligned in their goals, focusing on accountability and well-documented transactions.
steps.feedback: Ensure that the system not only records transactions but also has robust mechanisms for client verification and account protection. Additionally, consider refining the descriptions for clarity and comprehensibility.

kaos_elements.description: The system shall ensure responsibility (stakeholder transparency): The bank client must be able to deposit an amount to and withdraw an amount from his or her accounts using the bank application
kaos_elements.measurable_criteria: Clause-level quality requirement for stakeholder transparency

kaos_elements.name: Responsibility Requirement 3
kaos_elements.description: The system shall ensure responsibility (ethical compliance): Each transaction must be recorded, and the client must have the ability to review all transactions performed against a given account
kaos_elements.measurable_criteria: Clause-level quality requirement for ethical compliance

kaos_elements.name: Responsibility Requirement 4
kaos_elements.description: The system shall ensure responsibility (regulatory accountability): Recorded transactions must include the date, time, transaction type, amount and account balance after the transaction
kaos_elements.measurable_criteria: Clause-level quality requirement for regulatory accountability

kaos_elements.name: Responsibility Requirement 5
kaos_elements.description: The system shall ensure responsibility (stakeholder transparency): A bank client can have two types of accounts
kaos_elements.measurable_criteria: Clause-level quality requirement for stakeholder transparency

kaos_elements.name: Responsibility Requirement 6
kaos_elements.description: The system shall ensure responsibility (ethical compliance): A checking-account and a saving-account
kaos_elements.measurable_criteria: Clause-level quality requirement for ethical compliance

kaos_elements.name: Responsibility Requirement 7
kaos_elements.description: The system shall ensure responsibility (regulatory accountability): For each checking account, one related saving-account can exists
kaos_elements.measurable_criteria: Clause-level quality requirement for regulatory accountability

steps.analysis_text: The requirements for both responsibility and safety are aligned, focusing on ensuring that account transactions are properly managed and recorded. Each responsibility requirement complements a safety requirement, indicating a cohesive understanding of both accountability and hazard prevention in the banking application. However, further clarification may be necessary to ensure that all scenarios are adequately addressed, particularly around the handling of insufficient funds and account access.
steps.analysis: The requirements for both responsibility and safety are aligned, focusing on ensuring that account transactions are properly managed and recorded. Each responsibility requirement complements a safety requirement, indicating a cohesive understanding of both accountability and hazard prevention in the banking application. However, further clarification may be necessary to ensure that all scenarios are adequately addressed, particularly around the handling of insufficient funds and account access.
steps.feedback: The requirements are well-defined, but it would be beneficial to elaborate on the user experience during a failed transaction due to insufficient funds. Additionally, the verification process for access via PIN code needs more detail to ensure security protocols are robust.
